brief_notes: Highly accurate reproduction of all numerical claims from the source extraction. All benchmark values (EF1%=16.72, second-best=11.9), IC50 values with confidence intervals (Z8739902234: 1.32 uM [1.14-1.55], resting state: 13.1 [12.78-13.45], NaV1.5: 5.14 [4.74-5.55], hERG: 6.27 [5.27-7.37]), library sizes (~5.5B, ~4.1B), hit rates (14%, 44.4%), binding affinities (-6.81, -12.43 kcal/mol), and compute specs (3000 CPUs, 1 RTX2080) are all correct. No hallucinated numbers detected. The paper is well-organized with clear sections and informative tables. Writing is solid academic prose. Citations use BibTeX but many are placeholder keys (e.g., gpu_docking2023, materials_ai2023) that would not resolve to real references, which lowers the citations score. The paper covers all major findings comprehensively including both screening campaigns, benchmark results, crystallographic validation, and focused screening. Presentation includes three well-structured tables summarizing key results. Minor weaknesses: some citations appear fabricated, the methods section could be more detailed on specific force field parameters, and the 57-target count for CASF-2016 is inferred rather than explicitly stated in the source (though it is factually correct).
